title: Logout endpoint accepts CSRFable GET requests
reasoning: The logout route is exposed as a normal URL and LogoutView performs logout in response to GET. Because GET requests do not require a CSRF token and can be triggered cross-site by links, images, or redirects, another site can force an authenticated user to be logged out. This is a state-changing action and should require POST with CSRF protection.
reproduction: While logged in, visit or embed a request to the logout URL from any page; the server will execute LogoutView.get and clear the session.
recommendation: Require POST for logout, use Django's CSRF protection, and update templates to submit a POST form or use Django's built-in LogoutView with POST-only behavior.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Users/tests.py contains no tests, so there is no assertion that logout rejects GET or requires CSRF-protected POST.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that GET /logout/ does not log out the user and that POST /logout/ logs out only through the intended route.
minimumFixScope: Change Users.views.LogoutView to handle logout via POST only and update Users.urls/templates or callers accordingly.
